purpose: residual honest no-go map for fresh-B/fresh-C marginal-adaptive model

model_definitions.reduction_output: sympLPN reduction outputs (C,y) with C=BA and y=Cx+Be (paper lsn-core.tex, Sec. 5.2 / eq. around lines 884, 954)
model_definitions.open_problem_8: open:marginal-adaptive (paper lsn-core.tex lines 1232--1238): randomized adaptive B=g(A,R); I(x;y|C)=o(n) remains open
model_definitions.lem_m2_status: lem:m2 (paper lsn-core.tex lines 1166--1177) is heuristic/conditional; correlated noise Be lives in <=2n-dimensional subspace
model_definitions.shared_vs_fresh: shared-B (same B across rows/samples) is closed by stacked-rank rank(HY)<=2n; fresh-B/fresh-C is the residual

distinguishers.name: W=0 spike (min-syndrome-weight zero)
distinguishers.status: CLOSED as non-vanishing (B-agnostic lower bound only; actual Pr[W=0] approaches q_graph from above)
distinguishers.label: THEOREM (rate -> 0)

distinguishers.name: W-law (full low-weight tail of min-syndrome-weight)
distinguishers.status: NO-GO for asymptotic break (fixed-n TV grows with m but vanishes when normalised by n)
distinguishers.label: EVIDENCE/NO-GO

distinguishers.name: I(x;y|C) (conditional mutual information)
distinguishers.rate: per-sample I ~ 0.04--0.05 bits; does not approach H(x)=n
distinguishers.label: EVIDENCE/OPEN

distinguishers.name: k-sample augmented-matrix rank (fresh independent B per sample)
distinguishers.status: NO-GO for fixed-k structural rank tests
distinguishers.label: NO-GO (subadditivity)

distinguishers.name: Structured marginal-uniform SD sweep
distinguishers.rate: all structured families (UCS, block, parity) stay at or above baseline; no non-vanishing leak
distinguishers.label: NO-GO

distinguishers.name: Permutation-of-uniform fresh-B (distinct rows)
distinguishers.rate: below baseline at m=2,3,4; above at m=5,6; no monotonic asymptotic reduction
distinguishers.status: ESCALATE (first reducing direction observed) but NO-GO for asymptotic break
distinguishers.label: EVIDENCE/ESCALATE/NO-GO

honest_residual_statement: In the fresh-B/fresh-C model (independent B/C per sample), every tested distinguisher leaks at a rate that tends to 0 in n.  No tested structured marginal-uniform B family produces a non-vanishing asymptotic gap.  Permutation-of-uniform is the first family to move below the uniform-B baseline at small m, but the gap is O(1) fixed-n and crosses by m=6.  lem:m2 in the fresh model therefore remains OPEN.

paper_draft_map.title: Residual open-problem map: randomized marginal-adaptive linear reductions
rows: no tested distinguisher leaks at non-vanishing rate
all families >= baseline or fixed-n only
below baseline for m=2,3,4; crosses by m=6

guards.L1_exact_arithmetic: all numbers imported as string fractions; aggregator uses Fraction only
guards.L3_query_class: each row notes its query class / unrestricted status
guards.L4_comparison_distribution: LPN targets were matched-rate and untransformed in source tracks
guards.CLOSURE_GRADE: fixed-n evidence is not conflated with asymptotic rates; shared-B closure is stated separately
